name: Toast
description: Transient notification card (Figma 541:7521, component set 459:1298): 480px, radius 10, 12/16 padding, a soft shadow, and a per-state tinted fill with a 1px border — normal gray.10/gray.30, success success.5/50, danger error.5/50, info info.5/50, warning warning.5/50. A SemiBold 16px title composes with prefix-icon, suffix-text, suffix-icon (the close X by default), and a 14px caption. The react target adds the design-less toaster layer: one Toaster (position top-center, duration 3000ms, max 3 by default — six screen edges available) plus the imperative toast() API; per-toast manual disables auto-dismiss so only the X closes it. Multiple toasts collapse into a stack — the newest is shown in front at full size, older ones shrink 5% per step and peek 14px toward the screen edge; hovering or focusing the stack fans them out into a full-size gap layout and pauses auto-dismiss until the pointer/focus leaves. danger announces as an alert, other states as status.

slots.name: suffix-text
slots.description: Follow-up action text after the title, e.g. 실행 취소.

slots.name: suffix-icon
slots.description: Action icon after the title — the close X by default.

slots.name: caption
slots.description: Regular 14px extra line under the title.

props.name: onClose
props.description: Renders the close X and fires when it's pressed (web: closable attribute + podo-close event).

limitations: The toaster stack/queue is react-only; place podo-toast yourself.
Static card only; dismissal and the toaster queue need client code.
Card only — apps place it in their own overlay; no suffix-icon slot (onClose renders the X).

keyboard: Tab reaches the close X / action content
accessibility.focusManagement: Toasts never steal focus; announcements ride aria-live.
